description: Evaluate whether a course or learning program built usable mastery rather than superficial satisfaction.

examples: We need to understand whether learners can actually apply what they learned after the course.
I want feedback on whether our course content helped people perform better.

nodes.label: Learning goals clarity
nodes.description: Determine whether participants understood what the course was supposed to help them do.

nodes.label: Applied mastery
nodes.description: Understand whether learners can describe concrete behaviors, tasks, or decisions they can now perform.

nodes.label: Instruction quality
nodes.description: Assess which instructional moments, assets, or facilitation choices helped or hindered learning.

nodes.label: Practice friction
nodes.description: Capture where practice, exercises, or reinforcement broke down.

nodes.label: Transfer confidence
nodes.description: Measure confidence and remaining uncertainty about applying the learning in real settings.
